Will of Thomas Foster
memoryArtifacts.description: Image of the Last Will and Testament of Thomas Foster of Wendover, England.  He mentions by name his wife Elizabeth, son Thomas, and daughters Cristian, Elizabeth, and Susann

memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Peter Foster
memoryArtifacts.description: Will of Peter Foster was probated 13 July 1609.  In it he mentions by name his wife Ellen and children Richard, Thomas, Katherine Tompkins, Elizabeth, Margery Cooke, Isabel,  and Ales Skinner.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Foster於大約1579年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾圖維爾時，他的父親Peter Patricke Foster30歲，母親Helene Newton Greenway15歲。他於1606年11月10日與Elizabeth Seamer在英國巴金哈姆爾圖維爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於從1618年2月22日到1618年3月26日在英國巴金哈姆爾文多福，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾文多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Foster于大约1579年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾圖維爾时，他的父亲Peter Patricke Foster30岁，母亲Helene Newton Greenway15岁。他于1606年11月10日与Elizabeth Seamer在英国巴金哈姆爾圖維爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于从1618年2月22日到1618年3月26日在英国巴金哈姆爾文多福，埋葬在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾文多福。

nameMeanings.name: Foster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Forster   ‘worker in a forest’.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English foster ‘foster parent’ (Old English fōstre, a derivative of fōstrian ‘to nourish or rear’). But other explanations are equally or more likely.
English: from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors’; see Forster 2.

variants.name: Fewster
meanings: English (mainly East Yorkshire and Durham): occupational name from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English fu(y)ster ‘maker of saddletrees’. See also Foster .

variants.name: Forster
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Foster was born about 1579, in Turville, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Peter Patricke Foster, was 30 and his mother, Helene Newton Greenway, was 15. He married Elizabeth Seamer on 10 November 1606, in Turville, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died from 22 February 1618 to 26 March 1618, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, and was buried in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Foster est né environ 1579, à Turville, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Peter Patricke Foster, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Helene Newton Greenway, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Seamer le 10 novembre 1606, à Turville, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé du 22 février 1618 au 26 mars 1618, à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Fosterが約1579年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアトゥルヴィルで生まれた時，父Peter Patricke Fosterは30歳，母Helene Newton Greenwayは15歳であった。1606年11月10日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアトゥルヴィルでElizabeth Seamerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はから1618年2月22日まで1618年3月26日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウエンドバーで死去し，イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウエンドバーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Foster(이)가 대략 1579년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 터르빌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peter Patricke Foster(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Helene Newton Greenway(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1606년 11월 10일 영국 벅잉함실르 터르빌에서 Elizabeth Seamer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1618년 2월 22일부터 1618년 3월 26일까지 영국 벅잉함실르 웬도버에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 웬도버에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Foster nasceu aproximadamente 1579, em Turville, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Peter Patricke Foster, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Helene Newton Greenway, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Seamer em 10 de novembro de 1606, em Turville, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu de 22 de fevereiro de 1618 a 26 de março de 1618, em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Foster родился около 1579 г. в местности турвилл, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Peter Patricke Foster было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Helene Newton Greenway, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Seamer 10 ноября 1606 г. в местности турвилл, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер с 22 февраля 1618 по 26 марта 1618 в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия и был похоронен в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.
